audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for servant oath, hand-under-thigh formula, kinship marriage search, chesed/emet prayer, Rebekah's actions and consent, blessing formula, and Isaac/Rebekah meeting. Long narrative chapter kept complete with focused notes.

verses.restored: Abraham was old, advanced in days, and the Eternal had blessed Abraham in everything.

familiar.text: And Abraham was old, and well stricken in age: and the LORD had blessed Abraham in all things.

verses.restored: Abraham said to his servant, elder of his house, who ruled over all that was his, Place your hand under my thigh.

familiar.text: And Abraham said unto his eldest servant of his house... Put, I pray thee, thy hand under my thigh:

notes.title: Hand under thigh
notes.body: An oath gesture tied to lineage/body. The exact ritual background is debated.

verses.restored: I will make you swear by the Eternal, the Divine of the heavens and the Divine of the earth, that you will not take a woman for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites among whom I dwell.

familiar.text: And I will make thee swear by the LORD, the God of heaven, and the God of the earth...

verses.restored: But you shall go to my land and to my kindred, and take a woman for my son Isaac.

familiar.text: But thou shalt go unto my country, and to my kindred, and take a wife unto my son Isaac.

verses.restored: The servant said to him, Perhaps the woman will not be willing to follow me to this land. Shall I return your son to the land from which you came?

familiar.text: And the servant said unto him, Peradventure the woman will not be willing to follow me...

notes.title: Woman’s willingness
notes.body: The servant raises the possibility that the woman may not consent to come.

verses.restored: Abraham said to him, Guard yourself, lest you return my son there.

familiar.text: And Abraham said unto him, Beware thou that thou bring not my son thither again.

verses.restored: The Eternal, the Divine of the heavens, who took me from my father’s house and from the land of my kindred, who spoke to me and swore to me, saying, To your seed I will give this land, he will send his messenger before you, and you shall take a woman for my son from there.

familiar.text: The LORD God of heaven, which took me from my father's house...

notes.title: Messenger before you
notes.body: Abraham trusts divine guidance ahead of the servant’s journey.

verses.restored: But if the woman is not willing to follow you, then you shall be free from this oath of mine. Only do not return my son there.

familiar.text: And if the woman will not be willing to follow thee, then thou shalt be clear from this my oath...

notes.title: Consent condition repeated
notes.body: If the woman will not come, the servant is released. Isaac is not to be taken back.

verses.restored: The servant placed his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master and swore to him concerning this matter.

familiar.text: And the servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master...

verses.restored: The servant took ten camels from the camels of his master and went, with every good thing of his master in his hand. He rose and went to Aram-Naharaim, to the city of Nahor.

familiar.text: And the servant took ten camels of the camels of his master...

verses.restored: He made the camels kneel outside the city by the well of water, at evening time, the time when women who draw water go out.

familiar.text: And he made his camels to kneel down without the city by a well of water...

verses.restored: He said, Eternal, Divine of my master Abraham, please make it happen before me today, and show covenant-kindness to my master Abraham.

familiar.text: And he said, O LORD God of my master Abraham, I pray thee, send me good speed this day...

notes.title: Covenant-kindness
notes.body: Chesed is loyal, covenantal kindness. The servant prays for guided success as kindness to Abraham.

verses.restored: Look, I am standing by the spring of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.

familiar.text: Behold, I stand here by the well of water; and the daughters of the men of the city...

verses.restored: Let the young woman to whom I say, Please lower your jar so I may drink, and who says, Drink, and I will also water your camels, be the one you have appointed for your servant Isaac. By this I shall know that you have shown covenant-kindness to my master.

familiar.text: And let it come to pass, that the damsel to whom I shall say...

notes.title: Hospitality test
notes.body: The sign sought is active generosity beyond the request: water for the servant and camels.

verses.restored: Before he had finished speaking, look, Rebekah came out, who had been born to Bethuel son of Milcah, woman of Nahor, Abraham’s brother, and her jar was on her shoulder.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, before he had done speaking, that, behold, Rebekah came out...

verses.restored: The young woman was very good in appearance, a virgin, and no man had known her. She went down to the spring, filled her jar, and came up.

familiar.text: And the damsel was very fair to look upon, a virgin, neither had any man known her...

verses.restored: The servant ran to meet her and said, Please let me sip a little water from your jar.

familiar.text: And the servant ran to meet her, and said, Let me, I pray thee, drink a little water...

verses.restored: She said, Drink, my lord. She hurried and lowered her jar to her hand and gave him a drink.

familiar.text: And she said, Drink, my lord: and she hasted, and let down her pitcher...

verses.restored: When she finished giving him a drink, she said, I will also draw for your camels until they have finished drinking.

familiar.text: And when she had done giving him drink, she said, I will draw water for thy camels also...

verses.restored: She hurried and emptied her jar into the trough, ran again to the well to draw, and drew for all his camels.

familiar.text: And she hasted, and emptied her pitcher into the trough...

notes.title: Rebekah’s agency
notes.body: The narrative emphasizes Rebekah’s quick, active generosity.

verses.restored: The man was gazing at her in silence, to know whether the Eternal had made his journey successful or not.

familiar.text: And the man wondering at her held his peace, to wit whether the LORD had made his journey prosperous...

verses.restored: When the camels had finished drinking, the man took a gold nose-ring weighing a beka and two bracelets for her arms weighing ten shekels of gold.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, as the camels had done drinking...

verses.restored: He said, Whose daughter are you? Please tell me. Is there room in your father’s house for us to spend the night?

familiar.text: And said, Whose daughter art thou? tell me, I pray thee...

verses.restored: She said to him, I am the daughter of Bethuel, son of Milcah, whom she bore to Nahor.

familiar.text: And she said unto him, I am the daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah...

verses.restored: She also said to him, We have plenty of straw and feed, and also room to spend the night.

familiar.text: She said moreover unto him, We have both straw and provender enough, and room to lodge in.

verses.restored: The man bowed and worshiped the Eternal.

familiar.text: And the man bowed down his head, and worshipped the LORD.

verses.restored: He said, Blessed be the Eternal, Divine of my master Abraham, who has not abandoned covenant-kindness and truth toward my master. As for me, the Eternal has led me on the way to the house of my master’s brothers.

familiar.text: And he said, Blessed be the LORD God of my master Abraham...

notes.title: Covenant-kindness and truth
notes.body: Chesed ve-emet means loyal kindness and truth/faithfulness.

verses.restored: The young woman ran and told her mother’s house about these things.

familiar.text: And the damsel ran, and told them of her mother's house these things.

verses.restored: Rebekah had a brother, and his name was Laban. Laban ran out to the man, to the spring.

familiar.text: And Rebekah had a brother, and his name was Laban...

verses.restored: When he saw the nose-ring and the bracelets on his sister’s arms, and when he heard the words of Rebekah his sister, saying, Thus the man spoke to me, he came to the man; and look, he was standing by the camels at the spring.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when he saw the earring and bracelets...

verses.restored: He said, Come in, blessed of the Eternal. Why do you stand outside? I have prepared the house and a place for the camels.

familiar.text: And he said, Come in, thou blessed of the LORD...

verses.restored: The man came into the house, and he unloaded the camels. Straw and feed were given to the camels, and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men who were with him.

familiar.text: And the man came into the house: and he ungirded his camels...

verses.restored: Food was set before him to eat, but he said, I will not eat until I have spoken my words. He said, Speak.

familiar.text: And there was set meat before him to eat: but he said, I will not eat...

verses.restored: He said, I am Abraham’s servant.

familiar.text: And he said, I am Abraham's servant.

verses.restored: The Eternal has blessed my master greatly, and he has become great. He has given him flocks, herds, silver, gold, male servants, female servants, camels, and donkeys.

familiar.text: And the LORD hath blessed my master greatly; and he is become great...

verses.restored: Sarah, my master’s woman, bore a son to my master after she was old, and he has given him all that is his.

familiar.text: And Sarah my master's wife bare a son to my master when she was old...

verses.restored: My master made me swear, saying, You shall not take a woman for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites in whose land I dwell,

familiar.text: And my master made me swear, saying, Thou shalt not take a wife...

verses.restored: but you shall go to my father’s house and to my family, and take a woman for my son.

familiar.text: But thou shalt go unto my father's house, and to my kindred...

verses.restored: I said to my master, Perhaps the woman will not follow me.

familiar.text: And I said unto my master, Peradventure the woman will not follow me.

verses.restored: He said to me, The Eternal, before whom I have walked, will send his messenger with you and make your way successful. You shall take a woman for my son from my family and from my father’s house.

familiar.text: And he said unto me, The LORD, before whom I walk, will send his angel with thee...

verses.restored: Then you shall be free from my oath when you come to my family. If they do not give her to you, you shall be free from my oath.

familiar.text: Then shalt thou be clear from this my oath, when thou comest to my kindred...

verses.restored: I came today to the spring and said, Eternal, Divine of my master Abraham, if now you are making successful the way on which I go,

familiar.text: And I came this day unto the well, and said, O LORD God of my master Abraham...

verses.restored: look, I am standing by the spring of water. Let the maiden who comes out to draw, to whom I say, Please give me a little water from your jar to drink,

familiar.text: Behold, I stand by the well of water; and it shall come to pass...

verses.restored: and who says to me, You drink, and I will also draw for your camels—let her be the woman whom the Eternal has appointed for my master’s son.

familiar.text: And she say to me, Both drink thou, and I will also draw for thy camels...

verses.restored: Before I had finished speaking in my heart, look, Rebekah came out, with her jar on her shoulder. She went down to the spring and drew, and I said to her, Please let me drink.

familiar.text: And before I had done speaking in mine heart, behold, Rebekah came forth...

verses.restored: She hurried and lowered her jar from upon her shoulder and said, Drink, and I will also water your camels. So I drank, and she also watered the camels.

familiar.text: And she made haste, and let down her pitcher from her shoulder...

verses.restored: I asked her and said, Whose daughter are you? She said, The daughter of Bethuel, Nahor’s son, whom Milcah bore to him. Then I placed the ring on her nose and the bracelets on her arms.

familiar.text: And I asked her, and said, Whose daughter art thou?...

verses.restored: I bowed and worshiped the Eternal, and blessed the Eternal, Divine of my master Abraham, who led me in the true way to take the daughter of my master’s brother for his son.

familiar.text: And I bowed down my head, and worshipped the LORD...

verses.restored: Now if you are acting with covenant-kindness and truth toward my master, tell me; and if not, tell me, so that I may turn to the right hand or to the left.

familiar.text: And now if ye will deal kindly and truly with my master, tell me...

verses.restored: Laban and Bethuel answered and said, The matter has gone out from the Eternal; we cannot speak to you bad or good.

familiar.text: Then Laban and Bethuel answered and said, The thing proceedeth from the LORD...

verses.restored: Look, Rebekah is before you. Take her and go, and let her become the woman of your master’s son, as the Eternal has spoken.

familiar.text: Behold, Rebekah is before thee, take her, and go...

verses.restored: When Abraham’s servant heard their words, he bowed himself to the earth before the Eternal.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, that, when Abraham's servant heard their words...

verses.restored: The servant brought out objects of silver, objects of gold, and garments, and gave them to Rebekah. He also gave precious things to her brother and to her mother.

familiar.text: And the servant brought forth jewels of silver, and jewels of gold...

verses.restored: They ate and drank, he and the men who were with him, and spent the night. They rose in the morning, and he said, Send me away to my master.

familiar.text: And they did eat and drink, he and the men that were with him...

verses.restored: Her brother and her mother said, Let the young woman stay with us days, perhaps ten; afterward she may go.

familiar.text: And her brother and her mother said, Let the damsel abide with us a few days...

verses.restored: He said to them, Do not delay me, since the Eternal has made my way successful. Send me away, so that I may go to my master.

familiar.text: And he said unto them, Hinder me not, seeing the LORD hath prospered my way...

verses.restored: They said, Let us call the young woman and ask her mouth.

familiar.text: And they said, We will call the damsel, and enquire at her mouth.

notes.title: Ask her mouth
notes.body: Rebekah’s own answer is requested. This is a crucial consent moment in the narrative.

verses.restored: They called Rebekah and said to her, Will you go with this man? She said, I will go.

familiar.text: And they called Rebekah, and said unto her, Wilt thou go with this man? And she said, I will go.

notes.title: I will go
notes.body: Rebekah gives direct consent and chooses immediate departure.

verses.restored: They sent away Rebekah their sister, her nurse, Abraham’s servant, and his men.

familiar.text: And they sent away Rebekah their sister, and her nurse...

verses.restored: They blessed Rebekah and said to her, Our sister, may you become thousands of ten-thousands, and may your seed possess the gate of those who hate him.

familiar.text: And they blessed Rebekah, and said unto her, Thou art our sister, be thou the mother of thousands...

notes.title: Rebekah blessing
notes.body: The family blessing echoes the seed/gate promise language spoken over Abraham’s line.

verses.restored: Rebekah and her young women rose, rode on the camels, and followed the man. The servant took Rebekah and went.

familiar.text: And Rebekah arose, and her damsels, and they rode upon the camels...

verses.restored: Isaac had come from the way of Beer-lahai-roi, and he was dwelling in the land of the Negev.

familiar.text: And Isaac came from the way of the well Lahairoi...

notes.title: Beer-lahai-roi
notes.body: The well associated with Hagar reappears near Isaac’s story.

verses.restored: Isaac went out to meditate in the field toward evening. He lifted his eyes and saw, and look, camels were coming.

familiar.text: And Isaac went out to meditate in the field at the eventide...

notes.title: Meditate
notes.body: The verb suach is rare; meditate, stroll, or muse are possible.

verses.restored: Rebekah lifted her eyes and saw Isaac, and she dismounted from the camel.

familiar.text: And Rebekah lifted up her eyes, and when she saw Isaac, she lighted off the camel.

verses.restored: She said to the servant, Who is that man walking in the field to meet us? The servant said, He is my master. So she took the veil and covered herself.

familiar.text: For she had said unto the servant, What man is this that walketh in the field to meet us?...

verses.restored: The servant recounted to Isaac all the things that he had done.

familiar.text: And the servant told Isaac all things that he had done.

verses.restored: Isaac brought her into the tent of Sarah his mother. He took Rebekah, and she became his woman, and he loved her. Isaac was comforted after his mother.

familiar.text: And Isaac brought her into his mother Sarah's tent, and took Rebekah...

notes.title: Sarah’s tent
notes.body: Rebekah enters Sarah’s tent, symbolically moving into the matriarchal place after Sarah’s death.

notes.title: He loved her
notes.body: Love is explicitly named in Isaac and Rebekah’s union.
